verses.text: Seuls les hommes pieux peuvent résider dans la présence du Seigneur ( **5.4** ). En tant qu'invités, ils bénéficient de son hospitalité et de sa protection. •tente : à l'époque de David, le sanctuaire de Dieu est une tente (le tabernacle ; voir **1S 21.6** ; **2S 6.17** ; **7.6** ; **12.20** ; **1Ch 6.32** ).
Les hommes pieux parlent avec sincérité et agissent fidèlement dans leurs relations. Contrairement aux méchants, ils font preuve de franchise et de transparence ( **12.2-4** ).

15:3 He that backbiteth not with his tongue,.... Is not a slanderer, a defamer, a tale bearer; a backbiter is one who privately, secretly, behind a man's back speaks evil of him, devours and destroys his credit and reputation: the word here used comes from which signifies the "foot", and denotes such a person who goes about from house to house, speaking things he should not, 1 Tim 5:13; and a word from this root signifies spies; and the phrase here may point at such persons who creep into houses, pry into the secrets of families, and divulge them, and oftentimes represent them in a false light.

15:4 In whose eyes a vile person is contemned,.... A "vile" man is a very wicked, profligate, and abandoned creature, one that is to every good work reprobate; and such sometimes are in high places, Ps 12:8; and are greatly caressed and esteemed by the men of the world; but then, as they are an abomination to God, they should be despised by his people, let them be what they will as to their riches, honours, and wisdom among men; as Haman was by Mordecai, Est 3:2;

Le Seigneur a interdit à Israël d'exiger des intérêts des personnes dans le besoin ( **Ex 22.25-27** ). • L'assurance de la présence de Dieu garde les hommes pieux de la destruction ( **Ps 16.8 ** ; **46.5 ** ; **125.1** ). Leurs circonstances peuvent les ébranler temporairement, mais ils tiennent finalement fermes ( **30.6 ** ; **94.18 ** ; **112.6** ).
